page: Microsoft Azure E128-32ds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Edsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance features 32 dedicated vCPUs paired with 1024.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. The server is equipped with 7559 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations.

The hardware profile of this instance emphasizes massive memory capacity relative to compute resources. By allocating 1024.0 GB of RAM to 32 vCPUs, the configuration addresses workloads that require large in-memory datasets without needing the processing power of a full host. This resource density provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications, as users avoid paying for unneeded CPU cores while retaining access to a terabyte of RAM. However, the lack of dedicated GPU hardware limits its utility for parallel acceleration tasks.

This server type is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale relational databases, in-memory caches, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory footprints with moderate compute requirements.
description: Microsoft Azure E128-32ds_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine within the Edsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs and 1024.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high ratio of 32.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance includes 7559 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. This configuration is engineered for memory-bound workloads that do not require proportional scaling of compute power, offering qualitative cost efficiency by decoupling massive memory capacity from high vCPU counts. It is suited for large-memory databases, in-memory data analytics, and enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
